commentary: German day-ahead fair value prints soft at 55.2 EUR/MWh baseload and 65.3 EUR/MWh peak, -47.5 EUR/MWh versus the seasonal naive, with the 90% conformal band spanning [23.3, 87.1] EUR/MWh. Fundamentals are renewables-led: residual load averages 16694 MW against load 50274 MW, wind 31333 MW and solar 2247 MW, lifting renewable penetration to 66.8% and pushing gas and lignite units down the merit order. With renewables covering roughly two-thirds of load, the clearing price sits near the wind and solar segment rather than TTF-linked CCGT peakers, compressing the peak-base spread and reducing the value of thermal optionality on the prompt. Dominant regime is 1 (low); Dunkelflaute risk is 0.00 while negative-price risk is elevated at 0.63, consistent with high-renewable shoulder-hour setups that historically print toward deep negative hours in DE-LU. Model confidence remains constructive on the walk-forward record: skill versus naive is 22.7% at MAE 25.4 EUR/MWh, with SHAP still led by residual_load and conformal coverage near the 90% nominal target outside rare Dunkelflaute tails. Signal: SHORT prompt day.
